reasoning: The informal statement is an antiderivative claim: F(x) = ln|tan x - 2| - ln|tan x + 1| - 2 ln|cos x| + C is an antiderivative of the integrand, i.e., F'(x) = integralFunction(x). The Lean statement uses `∫ x, integralFunction x`, which by Mathlib's notation (Bochner.lean line 804) denotes `MeasureTheory.integral volume integralFunction` — the Bochner integral over all of ℝ, yielding a single real number, not an antiderivative. The inner `x` in `∫ x, ...` also shadows the outer ∀-bound `x`, so the LHS is a constant independent of the outer x. Since the precedence of `=` (50) is lower than `r:60`, the equation parses as (integral) = F(x) + C. The resulting statement "∀ x, ∃ C, (constant) = F(x) + C" is trivially true for any F and constant (pick C := constant - F(x)), so it is logically implied by the informal statement but captures none of its antiderivative content. A faithful formalization would use `HasDerivAt` or an interval integral.
